International Women’s Day Celebrations
Women Empowerment Cell
N O T I C E 02/03/2026
In celebration of International Women's Day, Women Empowerment Cell (WEC) is
organizing various competitions exclusively for girl students. We cordially invite all
interested girl students to participate in these competitions and showcase their talents. For
further information students may contact Dr.S.Indhumathi, Co-ordinator of WEC (cell
no:8985121344) & Dr,B.Vasavi, Co-coordinator of WEC (cell no:9493238155).
The competitions scheduled for International Women's Day include:
1. Pencil Art
Date & Time: 5/3/2026 @9:30A.M. Rules and Regulations:
1. Participants have to use A4 size paper
Venue: Boys waiting shed near workshop sheets for drawing.
2. Participants need to bring A4 sheets
Judges: and other material required.
1. Smt. M. Sujana 3. Time limit is 90 minutes.
2. Smt. P. Chandrakala
2. Rangoli
Date & Time: 5/3/2026 @10:00A.M. Rules and Regulations:
1. Theme : “Women Empowerment”
Venue: Boys waiting shed near workshop 2. Participants need to bring rangoli
material and other accessories
Judges: 3. Maximum of 2 members is allowed in a
1. Dr.G.Kameswari team.
2. Dr.K.Radika 4. Time Limit is 90 minutes
3. Solo Singing
Date & Time: 6/3/2026@1:30 P.M Rules and Regulations:
1. Time allotted for each performance is
Venue: ME Seminar Hall 2-3 minutes.
2. Participants will not be allowed to
Judges: refer to the lyrics while singing.
1. Dr. P. Vijaya Jyothi 3. Choice of song is open to the
2. Smt. J. Charishma participants but the song should not
have any slangs or derogatory
language.
4. Solo Dancing
Date & Time: 6/3/2024@1:30P.M Rules and Regulations:
Venue: ME Seminar Hall 1. Time limit is 2-3 minutes.
Judges: 2. It could be just one song or a medley
1. Dr. P. Dhana Selvi of songs on any theme.
2. Smt. J. Charishma

5. e Poster Presentation
Date& Time: 6/3/2026 @ 3P.M Rules and Regulations:
1. Create your poster using MS-Power
Venue: ME Seminar Hall Point, using a single slide. size :
1366 x 768 (.jpeg)
Judges: 2. Focus on themes like "Women's
1. Dr. N.Usha Empowerment" "Women's Safety"
2. Dr.R.Lavanya or "Gender Equality".
3. Time limit is 3–5 minutes for
presentation followed by 2–5
minutes for Q&A.
4. Maximum 2 participants are allowed
per poster.
6. Mehendi Design Competition
Date & Time : 06/03/2026 @2 P.M. Rules and Regulations:
1. Mehendi design must cover one hand
Venue: HT Lab ( Near ME Seminar Hall) and also must be covering both sides
of the palm (Palmer as well as the
Judges: dorsal side of the hand).
1. Dr. Y. Chitra Rekha 2. Participants need to bring mehendi
2. Dr. M. Harita Cones
3. Participants can apply mehendi on
their own hand or any other person’s
hand.
4. Time Limit is one hour.
Note: 1. Registration fee for each event per participant is ₹ 50.
2. Participants need to report at the venue 30 minutes before
the scheduled time for registration.

Sub:- Extension of Autonomous Status to N.B.K.R. Institute of Science & Technology,
Vidyanagar, Kota-Mandal, Tirupati-Dist. A.P. affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru Technological
University, Anantapur
Sir/Madam,
This has reference to the proposal submitted by N.B.K.R. Institute of Science &
Technology, Vidyanagar, Kota-Mandal, Tirupati-Dist. A.P. affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru
Technological University, Anantapur for extension of Autonomous Status.
The Commission in its meeting held on 25.06.2024 has approved the recommendation of
the Standing Committee on Autonomous Colleges to extend the autonomous status to
N.B.K.R. Institute of Science & Technology, Vidyanagar, Kota-Mandal, Tirupati-Dist. A.P.
affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Ananatpur for a period of 10 years
from the academic year 2024-2025 to 2033-2034 as per clause 8.2 of the UGC (Conferment of
Autonomous Status Upon Colleges and Measures for Maintenance of Standards in Autonomous
Colleges) Regulations, 2023.
The University, is therefore, requested to issue necessary notification within 30 days
regarding the extension of autonomous status to the College as per UGC (Conferment of
Autonomous Status Upon Colleges and Measures for Maintenance of Standards in Autonomous
Colleges) Regulations, 2023.
The autonomous college is required to abide by all the provisions of the UGC Regulations
for Autonomous Colleges. The Regulations are available on the UGC website, www.ugc.gov.in.
Non-compliance of the requirements and conditions prescribed in the said Regulations shall
attract action as per Clause -13 of the UGC (Conferment of Autonomous Status Upon Colleges
and Measures for Maintenance of Standards in Autonomous Colleges) Regulations, 2023. The
college should apply to University Grants Commission for extension of autonomous status at least
three months before the completion of autonomy period.
Yours faithfully,

1. The Principal Secretary,

Sree Mahendra Enclave, NRI Block (C-Block)
I & II Floors, Opposite State Bank of India
Adjacent to NH-16, Tadepalli, Guntur-522 501
2. The Principal,
N.B.K.R. Institute of Science & Technology,
Vidyanagar, Kota-Mandal,
Tirupati-Dist. A.P.
(i) The College is advised to go for the required NAAC/NBA accreditation at least six
months before the expiry of current validity and obtain the grading NAAC/NBA as per
the UGC (Conferment of Autonomous Status Upon Colleges and Measures for
Maintenance of Standards in Autonomous Colleges) Regulations, 2023 and intimate
the Same to UGC to revisit the above decision, if required.
(ii) The College/lnstitute is advised to submit a report regarding Examination Cell and
constitution of Statutory Bodies as required under Clauses 11 and 12 of the UGC
(Conferment of Autonomous Status Upon Colleges and Measures for Maintenance
of Standards in Autonomous Colleges) Regulations, 2023 to ensure proper
management of academic, financial and general administrative affairs. Non
compliance ofthe above shall attract action as per Clause -13 ofthe UGC Regulations
- 2023.
(iii) The College/lnstitute is advised to take necessary steps for implementation of NEP
2020 and intimate the UGC.
